mnemonics: G.O.M.S. - Gradients Of Matrices are Sums (of outer products for vector derivatives)
keyPoints: The gradient of a matrix with respect to a vector is a higher-order tensor (3D array)
For a matrix A and vector x, ∂A/∂x results in a tensor where each element (i,j,k) is ∂A_ij/∂x_k
The derivative of a matrix function f(A) often uses the vec operator and Kronecker products to transform into a vector gradient problem
commonMistakes: Treating matrix gradients as simple 2D arrays instead of 3D tensors
Forgetting that ∂(AX)/∂x requires product rule expansion when A is a function of x
Confusing the layout convention (numerator vs denominator layout) for matrix derivatives
